bead.title: server: expose worker desired-state control through GraphQL and CLI
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Operators need a first-class way to monitor, start, stop, restart, reconcile, and set worker desired count through the DDx server. Today the server exposes one-shot start/stop, and routine management still falls back to direct ddx work or tmux-like process control.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1931 StartWorkerInput covers a one-shot start spec but not desired_count or restart policy. schema.graphql:3271-3285 exposes workerDispatch, startWorker, and stopWorker, but no set desired state, restart worker, or reconcile mutation. cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:89 StartWorker dispatches work once through ActionDispatcher. cli/internal/server/server.go:2568 handles POST /api/agent/workers/work as a one-shot worker start. There is no ddx worker command group in cli/cmd for server-backed lifecycle control.

PROPOSED FIX
Add GraphQL/server operations and CLI commands for managed worker control: set desired state, status, start, stop, restart, reconcile, and cleanup. workersByProject or successor status output must identify managed vs reported external workers, desired vs actual count, restart count, last restart/exit reason, and process group id. Reported external workers must not expose stop/restart as if the server owns them.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build frontend UI in this bead. Do not implement Docker E2E here. Do not remove direct ddx work; keep it as an explicit escape hatch.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestGraphQLSetWorkerDesiredState persists desired_count/default spec/restart policy and triggers reconcile for the requested project.
2. TestGraphQLRestartWorker stops and restarts a managed worker while preserving lifecycle evidence and restart_count.
3. TestGraphQLWorkersDistinguishManagedAndExternal proves external reported workers are marked managed=false and do not expose server-owned stop/restart capability.
4. TestWorkerCLISetStatusRestartReconcile exercises ddx worker set/status/restart/reconcile against a test server without starting real Claude/Codex.
5. ddx worker --help documents status, set, start, stop, restart, reconcile, and cleanup commands.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestGraphQLSetWorkerDesiredState|TestGraphQLRestartWorker|TestGraphQLWorkersDistinguishManagedAndExternal|TestWorkerCLISetStatusRestartReconcile' passes.
7. make install is run after the code change lands.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

However, open bead ddx-9d547009 documents a deterministic data race in cli/internal/server/provider_probe_cleanup.go that causes 'go test -short -race ./internal/server' to fail on every run. scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh runs exactly that command for each staged package; internal/server will be staged by this bead's changes.
